steps.dialogue: Let's take a moment to think about what we have learned in this lesson.

You learned two different ways to compare fractions. We covered the fact that [fraction numerator=1 denominator=4]one fourth[/fraction] was bigger than [fraction numerator=1 denominator=8]one eighth[/fraction] even though 8 is a bigger number. We had to learn to think about what those numbers really mean when they're denominators in a fraction.

You learned to check what matches first - denominators or numerators - and then choose the right strategy to compare their size. You're making decisions about which tool to use to solve different types of problems.
You worked through some challenging ideas and problems. Good work today.
